MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION
Off Calendar Oct-26-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:

Dan M. Hammilheber, Esq., for Kevin Bellad, Plaintiff/Moving Party.
Charles M. Collins, Esq., for Lizzie Rae Young, for Defendant/Opposing Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiff Kevin Bellard's Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony At Oral Deposition Of Lizzie Rae young and To Delay Plaintiff's Deposition is denied. All requests for sanctions are denied.
Modification: Modified to include the provision that Ms. Young may not testify at trial untill and unless she is deposed. At this time the court finds that she is incompetent to give testimony. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-24-2006 Text Ruling

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-09-2006 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel And To Impose Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-04-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Charles Michael Collins, Esq., for Lizzie Young and Aleene Young, Defendant and Moving Party.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendants' Motion To Compel Attendance And Testimony And Production Of Documents At Oral Deposition Of Kevin Bellard is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to appear at his deposition and to produce documents on a date to be noticed by Defendant, but no later than October 27, 2006. The court does not address the possible Fifth Amendment issues that may be raised by particular deposition questions. Plaintiff's failure to appear was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $640, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION
Off Calendar Sep-14-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Charles M. Collins, Esq., for Defendant and Moving Party Alene Young

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Creditors Trade Association's Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2006 Set For Trial On Oct-30-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Oct-12-06 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 606
